title: Medical Assisting Instructor
description:
Job Summary

Responsible for providing instruction in accordance with the philosophy and objectives of the College.

Essential Functions

Adhere to approved course syllabus.

Provide students with the course syllabus that informs them of course requirements including specific objectives, means of attaining those objectives and methods of evaluation by the end of the first week of classes.

Use teaching methods which are appropriate to reach students of varying educational experiential backgrounds and learning styles. Allow the student to continually assess his/her own progress. Allow reasonable opportunity for each student to master the objectives of the course.

Submit syllabi and/or addendums to the Division Office by the end of the first week of class. Post and maintain office hours as per institutional guidelines. Make provisions for students to evaluate instruction and arrange for submission of evaluation to the designated office.

Participate in faculty orientation sessions, professional development seminars and activities to enhance professional development. Accept the responsibility for professional awareness and growth. Acknowledge and support the community college's mission to serve the community.

Perform all other duties appropriate to this position as assigned or directed.

Qualifications

Required Qualifications:

1. Associate's degree in Medical Assisting or Nursing.

NOTE: A copy of the transcript reflecting this required education MUST be submitted for consideration and reflect completed coursework and degrees conferred. The candidate must be fully qualified for the position at the time of application.

2. Three (3) years of work related experience exclusive of teaching.

3. Must have one or more of the following:
a. CMA (Certified Medical Assistant)
b. RN (Registered Nurse)
c. LVN (Licensed Vocational Nurse)
d. MD (Medical Doctor)

4. Experience teaching in the discipline.

5. Experience/ability to incorporate technology into the classroom.

6. Experience/ability to teach at the community college level.

Special Conditions:

1. The El Paso Community College is building a pool of Applicants for part-time faculty. Faculty are hired in a part-time capacity on a semester-by-semester basis, contingent upon the needs of the District. EPCC accepts application materials year-round from qualified applicants willing to teach on a part-time basis. Part-time faculty hiring is based on college need. We offer day, evening, and weekend classes. Classes are offered at various times and locations during the academic year. This pool will be accessed when an opening arises. Your application will remain active for approximately one (1) year from July 2025 to June 2026. After this time, you will need to re-apply if you are still interested in remaining a part of the pool.

2. This is a security-sensitive position as defined under the Texas Education Code, Section 51.215; the successful applicant will be required to undergo a criminal background check, as permitted and/or required by applicable law, and in accordance with the College’s policies and procedures.

Other Requirements

Individuals desiring consideration MUST complete and submit an EPCC Application for Employment on-line by the announced deadline.

COPIES OF ACADEMIC TRANSCRIPTS MUST BE uploaded with the application. CURRENT EMPLOYEES ARE ALSO REQUIRED TO UPLOAD THEIR TRANSCRIPTS WITH EACH SUBMITTED APPLICATION; TRANSCRIPTS IN PERSONNEL FILES CANNOT BE ACCESSED BY EVALUATORS FOR THIS PURPOSE. APPLICATIONS SUBMITTED WITHOUT TRANSCRIPTS WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED. Only documents stating 'Transcript' or 'Unofficial Transcript' will be accepted. Other documents (Degree Audit, Degree Summary, Program Summary, etc.), uploaded in lieu of transcripts will not be accepted.

If you are unable to attach your transcripts to your application, please mail your transcripts to El Paso Community College, Employment Services, P.O. Box 20500, El Paso, TX 79998. Mailed documents MUST include the position title and posting number for this position vacancy.

A letter of application, resume, letters of recommendation, and other supporting documents are optional but highly encouraged and may be submitted on-line with the application form or forwarded to the Human Resources Department at El Paso Community College, Employment Services, P.O. Box 20500, El Paso, TX 79998. Mailed documents must be received by the application deadline. Documents received after the application deadline will not be accepted.

Copies of transcripts must reflect completed coursework and conferred degrees. All academic coursework and degrees must be from an accredited college or university or be certified by an acceptable agency that the institution is recognized by the appropriate governmental agency in its home country (this is generally stated in the degree evaluation).

Transcripts issued in a language other than English must be accompanied by a full translation (word for word) by a current member of the National Association of Credential Evaluation Services (NACES). Additionally, each foreign transcript must be evaluated for equivalency to United States accredited coursework.

Applications containing foreign transcripts that are not accompanied with the above required documentation will not be considered.

In accordance with federal law and as a condition of employment, the successful candidate must furnish documentation verifying employment authorization eligibility and identity before being employed.

The El Paso County Community College District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, religion, sex, age, disability or veteran status.

title: Lead Technician Low Voltage/Network Cabling - El Paso, TX
description:
Overview Take the lead in delivering high-quality low voltage and network cabling solutions with All Ether LLC! We’re looking for a skilled and motivated Lead Technician to oversee installations, manage teams, and ensure the successful deployment of Wi-Fi networks, fire alarm systems, security cameras, access control systems, and telecommunications setups. As a Lead Technician, you will play a key role in guiding technicians, troubleshooting complex installations, and ensuring project timelines are met with precision. If you're a problem solver, strong leader, and highly skilled technician, this is your chance to step up and make an impact.

About All Ether LLC

All Ether is a women-owned technology solutions provider based in Nashville, Tennessee, with over 50 years of combined experience in the industry. Specializing in advanced networking, cabling infrastructure, and CCTV systems, we deliver tailored network infrastructure solutions that empower businesses to stay connected and secure. At All Ether, we excel in our ability to deliver comprehensive and customized technology solutions that meet the unique needs of your business.

Description

Ideal Candidate Profile

✔ Experienced Leader: You have 4+ years of experience in low voltage/network cabling, with expertise in structured cabling for Wi-Fi, fire alarms, cameras, access control, and telecom.

✔ Mentor & Trainer: You can guide, train, and support technicians while ensuring quality control and safety compliance.

✔ Problem-Solving Pro: You tackle challenges with logic and innovative solutions to keep projects on track.

✔ Background Considerations: We believe in second chances and review each candidate individually based on project requirements.

Key Responsibilities

Team Leadership: Oversee technicians, provide training, and ensure high-quality installations.

Project Execution: Manage and install Wi-Fi, fire alarms, cameras, access control, and telecom systems while meeting deadlines and client expectations.

Cable Management: Organize and manage CAT5, CAT6, fiber optic, and coaxial cables for functionality and efficiency.

Client Relations: Maintain clear and professional communication with clients and stakeholders.

Problem-Solving: Troubleshoot complex installations and ensure best practices are followed on every project.

Requirements

✔ Valid Driver’s License – You may be assigned a company vehicle, receive mileage reimbursement, or need reliable transportation to job sites.

✔ Ability to Travel – Some projects may require travel.

✔ Strong Organizational & Leadership Skills – Ability to manage teams, follow structured processes, and enforce safety protocols.

✔ Hands-on Experience – Expert knowledge of structured cabling, fiber optics, and telecom infrastructure.

✔ Clear Background Check – Must be able to pass a background screening.

✔ U.S. Citizenship or Legal Work Authorization – Required for access to certain job sites and contracts.

What We Offer

✅ Tools for Success: We equip all team members with the necessary tools to excel.

✅ Career Growth: We invest in leadership development and training for long-term career advancement.

✅ Flexible Schedules: Typically 5x 8-hour shifts per week, with potential for 4x 10-hour shifts or overtime based on project needs.

✅ Paid Time Off: Enjoy competitive benefits, including PTO to support work-life balance.

Join Our Team

This is more than just a job—it’s an opportunity to lead, mentor, and make a lasting impact in a company that values skill, leadership, and professionalism. If you're driven, experienced, and ready to take on a leadership role, we want to hear from you!

title: Operating Room Registered Nurse -El Paso TX
description:
Company Description

ProSidian is a Management and Operations Consulting Services Firm focusing on providing value to clients through tailored solutions based on industry-leading practices. ProSidian services focus on the broad spectrum of Enterprise Solutions for Risk Management | Compliance | Business Process | IT Effectiveness | Energy & Sustainability | Human Capital. We help forward-thinking clients solve problems and improve operations.

Launched by former Big 4 Management Consultants; our multidisciplinary teams bring together the talents of nearly 190 professionals nationally to complete a wide variety of engagements for Private Companies, Fortune 1,000 Enterprises, and Government Agencies of all sizes. Our Services are deployed across the enterprise, target drivers of economic profit (growth, margin and efficiency), and are aligned at the intersections of assets, processes, policies and people delivering value.

ProSidian clients represent a broad spectrum of industries to include but are not limited to Energy, Manufacturing, Chemical, Retail, Healthcare, Telecommunications, Hospitality, Pharmaceuticals, Banking & Financial Services, Transportation, Federal and State Government Agencies. Learn More About ProSidian Consulting at www.ProSidian.com.

Job Description

Project Profile

The Veterans Integrated Services Network 17 (VISN 17) is a large network comprised of seven (7) healthcare systems geographically dispersed throughout the state of Texas. The VISN is supporting expanded bed capacity to meet the increased operational surge capacity throughout the state of Texas, and to support the VA's fourth mission by providing humanitarian assistance. Based on the current and projected future workload, additional staffing will be required over the next 24 months to support seven healthcare systems.

The Candidate

ProSidian seeks a Registered Nurse to support an engagement with The Veterans Integrated Services Network 17 (VISN 17). This position supports the current expanded operations and post-COVID operations to restore the healthcare systems to pre-Pandemic operations.

This is a Contract Contingent position and the Registered Nurse services shall be performed at the: El Paso VA Health Care System- 5001 N Piedras St, El Paso, TX 79930.

Services Task and Job Requirements

In order to perform the duties set forth for this project, the Registered Nurse must meet both the minimum education and experience requirements set forth:

Qualifications

Qualifications

Education:

Graduated with a Bachelor of Science Nursing degree from a program that has been accredited by the Accreditation Commission for Education in Nursing (ACEN) or the Commission on Collegiate Nursing Education (CCNE).

Licensure:

Registered Nurse with a current Texas State license including BLS certification and ACLS certification.

Experience:
• Demonstrate leadership in delivering and improving holistic care through collaborative strategies with others.
• Demonstrate proficiency using the nursing process in providing care for the clients with complex nursing care needs and be able to guide and direct others who provide care.
• Apply the nursing process to systems or processes at the unit/team/work group level to improve care.
• Identify potential client problems and take appropriate nursing measures to intervene.
• Function effectively in a team leader/leadership role and uses team approaches to identify, analyze and resolve problems.
• Assess, plan, implement and evaluate the delivery of client care consistent with accrediting/regulatory bodies, the VA, and Nursing Service mission, philosophy, goals, and standards.
• Supervise other personnel and give feedback on their performance.
• Demonstrate ability to work effectively with clients, families, or significant others, with professional and supportive personnel who provide client care and with members of other services.
• Assess the physiological and psychological health status of the patient pre-operatively
• Formulates and implements a nursing plan of care based on health data and surgical procedure and posting.
• Plan of care that prescribes nursing actins to achieve patient goals and coordinates and organizes nursing activities.
• Coordinates patient care needs with team members, including other disciplines.
• Positioning patient properly for surgery.
• Creates and maintains a sterile field.
• Verify methods of sterility and safely use flash autoclaves.
• Receive, handle, store and document prostheses. Performs surgical hand scrub, create and maintain sterile fields.
• Provide equipment, supplies and instruments based on anticipated and unanticipated needs.
• Uses and care for surgical instruments, supplies and equipment safely and appropriately.
• Safe use and document of electro surgery unit.
• Performs and documents counts.
• Administer drugs and solutions as prescribed.
• Physiologically monitors the patient during procedures.
• Monitors and controls the Operating Room environment.
• Adheres to infection standards
• Performs nursing actions that demonstrates accountability.
• Participate in patient and family teaching.
• Performs and documents universal protocol.
• Handle and care for specimens and cultures on an off the sterile.
• Safely handle and transport medical gases and hazardous materials.
• Performs circulating and scrub duties for general, urology, vascular, neuro, plastic, orthopedic, ophthalmology, pain management, oral, robotic, thoracic, and otolaryngology surgeries.
• Must be able to demonstrate effective oral and written communication skills and tact in dealing with patients, families and staff.
• Must be able to demonstrate proficiency in keyboarding and basic computer operations.
• Must have the physical capabilities to initiate crisis intervention measures and the ability and knowledge to perform nursing care in an emergency.

Additional duties may be assigned. Training will be provided if needed.

Additional Information

CORE COMPETENCIES
• Teamwork - ability to foster teamwork collaboratively as a participant, and effectively as a team leader
• Leadership - ability to guide and lead colleagues on projects and initiatives
• Business Acumen - understanding and insight into how organizations perform, including business processes, data, systems, and people
• Communication - ability to effectively communicate to stakeholders of all levels orally and in writing
• Motivation - persistent in pursuit of quality and optimal client and company solutions
• Agility - ability to quickly understand and transition between different projects, concepts, initiatives, or work streams
• Judgment - exercises prudence and insight in decision-making process while mindful of other stakeholders and long-term ramifications
• Organization - ability to manage projects and activity, and prioritize tasks

------------ ------------ ------------

OTHER REQUIREMENTS
• Business Tools - understanding and proficiency with business tools and technology, including Microsoft Office. The ideal candidate is advanced with Excel, Access, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word, and proficient with Adobe Acrobat, data analytic tools, and Visio with the ability to quickly learn other tools as necessary.
• Business Tools - understanding and proficiency with business tools and technology, including Microsoft Office. The ideal candidate is advanced with Excel, Access, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word, and proficient with Adobe Acrobat, data analytic tools, and Visio with the ability to quickly learn other tools as necessary.
• Commitment - to work with smart, interesting people with diverse backgrounds to solve the biggest challenges across private, public and social sectors
• Curiosity - the ideal candidate exhibits an inquisitive nature and the ability to question the status quo among a community of people they enjoy and teams that work well together
• Humility - exhibits grace in success and failure while doing meaningful work where skills have impact and make a difference
• Willingness - to constantly learn, share, and grow and to view the world as their classroom

------------ ------------ ------------

BENEFITS AND HIGHLIGHTS

ProSidian Employee Benefits and Highlights: Your good health and well-being are important to ProSidian Consulting. At ProSidian, we invest in our employees to help them stay healthy and achieve work-life balance. That's why we are also pleased to offer the Employee Benefits Program, designed to promote your health and personal welfare. Our growing list of benefits currently include the following for Full Time Employees:

Competitive Compensation: Pay range begins in the competitive ranges with Group Health Benefits, Pre-tax Employee Benefits, and Performance Incentives. For medical and dental benefits, the Company contributes a fixed dollar amount each month towards the plan you elect. Contributions are deducted on a Pre-tax basis.

Group Medical Health Insurance Benefits: ProSidian partners with BC/BS, to offer a range of medical plans, including high-deductible health plans or PPOs. ||| Group Dental Health Insurance Benefits: ProSidian dental carriers - Delta, Aetna, Guardian, and MetLife.

Group Vision Health Insurance Benefits:ProSidian offers high/low vision plans through 2 carriers: Aetna and VSP.

401(k) Retirement Savings Plan: 401(k) Retirement Savings Plans help you save for your retirement for eligible employees. A range of investment options are available with a personal financial planner to assist you. The Plan is a pre-tax Safe Harbor 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan with a company match.

Vacation and Paid Time-Off (PTO) Benefits: Eligible employees use PTO for vacation, a doctor's appointment, or any number of events in your life. Currently these benefits include Vacation/Sick days - 2 weeks/3 days | Holidays - 10 ProSidian and Government Days are given.

raw_payload.job_title: Operating Room Registered Nurse -El Paso TX
raw_payload.job_description:
Company Description

ProSidian is a Management and Operations Consulting Services Firm focusing on providing value to clients through tailored solutions based on industry-leading practices. ProSidian services focus on the broad spectrum of Enterprise Solutions for Risk Management | Compliance | Business Process | IT Effectiveness | Energy & Sustainability | Human Capital. We help forward-thinking clients solve problems and improve operations.

Launched by former Big 4 Management Consultants; our multidisciplinary teams bring together the talents of nearly 190 professionals nationally to complete a wide variety of engagements for Private Companies, Fortune 1,000 Enterprises, and Government Agencies of all sizes. Our Services are deployed across the enterprise, target drivers of economic profit (growth, margin and efficiency), and are aligned at the intersections of assets, processes, policies and people delivering value.

ProSidian clients represent a broad spectrum of industries to include but are not limited to Energy, Manufacturing, Chemical, Retail, Healthcare, Telecommunications, Hospitality, Pharmaceuticals, Banking & Financial Services, Transportation, Federal and State Government Agencies. Learn More About ProSidian Consulting at www.ProSidian.com.

Job Description

Project Profile

The Veterans Integrated Services Network 17 (VISN 17) is a large network comprised of seven (7) healthcare systems geographically dispersed throughout the state of Texas. The VISN is supporting expanded bed capacity to meet the increased operational surge capacity throughout the state of Texas, and to support the VA's fourth mission by providing humanitarian assistance. Based on the current and projected future workload, additional staffing will be required over the next 24 months to support seven healthcare systems.

The Candidate

ProSidian seeks a Registered Nurse to support an engagement with The Veterans Integrated Services Network 17 (VISN 17). This position supports the current expanded operations and post-COVID operations to restore the healthcare systems to pre-Pandemic operations.

This is a Contract Contingent position and the Registered Nurse services shall be performed at the: El Paso VA Health Care System- 5001 N Piedras St, El Paso, TX 79930.

Services Task and Job Requirements

In order to perform the duties set forth for this project, the Registered Nurse must meet both the minimum education and experience requirements set forth:

Qualifications

Qualifications

Education:

Graduated with a Bachelor of Science Nursing degree from a program that has been accredited by the Accreditation Commission for Education in Nursing (ACEN) or the Commission on Collegiate Nursing Education (CCNE).

Licensure:

Registered Nurse with a current Texas State license including BLS certification and ACLS certification.

Experience:
• Demonstrate leadership in delivering and improving holistic care through collaborative strategies with others.
• Demonstrate proficiency using the nursing process in providing care for the clients with complex nursing care needs and be able to guide and direct others who provide care.
• Apply the nursing process to systems or processes at the unit/team/work group level to improve care.
• Identify potential client problems and take appropriate nursing measures to intervene.
• Function effectively in a team leader/leadership role and uses team approaches to identify, analyze and resolve problems.
• Assess, plan, implement and evaluate the delivery of client care consistent with accrediting/regulatory bodies, the VA, and Nursing Service mission, philosophy, goals, and standards.
• Supervise other personnel and give feedback on their performance.
• Demonstrate ability to work effectively with clients, families, or significant others, with professional and supportive personnel who provide client care and with members of other services.
• Assess the physiological and psychological health status of the patient pre-operatively
• Formulates and implements a nursing plan of care based on health data and surgical procedure and posting.
• Plan of care that prescribes nursing actins to achieve patient goals and coordinates and organizes nursing activities.
• Coordinates patient care needs with team members, including other disciplines.
• Positioning patient properly for surgery.
• Creates and maintains a sterile field.
• Verify methods of sterility and safely use flash autoclaves.
• Receive, handle, store and document prostheses. Performs surgical hand scrub, create and maintain sterile fields.
• Provide equipment, supplies and instruments based on anticipated and unanticipated needs.
• Uses and care for surgical instruments, supplies and equipment safely and appropriately.
• Safe use and document of electro surgery unit.
• Performs and documents counts.
• Administer drugs and solutions as prescribed.
• Physiologically monitors the patient during procedures.
• Monitors and controls the Operating Room environment.
• Adheres to infection standards
• Performs nursing actions that demonstrates accountability.
• Participate in patient and family teaching.
• Performs and documents universal protocol.
• Handle and care for specimens and cultures on an off the sterile.
• Safely handle and transport medical gases and hazardous materials.
• Performs circulating and scrub duties for general, urology, vascular, neuro, plastic, orthopedic, ophthalmology, pain management, oral, robotic, thoracic, and otolaryngology surgeries.
• Must be able to demonstrate effective oral and written communication skills and tact in dealing with patients, families and staff.
• Must be able to demonstrate proficiency in keyboarding and basic computer operations.
• Must have the physical capabilities to initiate crisis intervention measures and the ability and knowledge to perform nursing care in an emergency.

Additional duties may be assigned. Training will be provided if needed.

Additional Information

CORE COMPETENCIES
• Teamwork - ability to foster teamwork collaboratively as a participant, and effectively as a team leader
• Leadership - ability to guide and lead colleagues on projects and initiatives
• Business Acumen - understanding and insight into how organizations perform, including business processes, data, systems, and people
• Communication - ability to effectively communicate to stakeholders of all levels orally and in writing
• Motivation - persistent in pursuit of quality and optimal client and company solutions
• Agility - ability to quickly understand and transition between different projects, concepts, initiatives, or work streams
• Judgment - exercises prudence and insight in decision-making process while mindful of other stakeholders and long-term ramifications
• Organization - ability to manage projects and activity, and prioritize tasks

------------ ------------ ------------

OTHER REQUIREMENTS
• Business Tools - understanding and proficiency with business tools and technology, including Microsoft Office. The ideal candidate is advanced with Excel, Access, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word, and proficient with Adobe Acrobat, data analytic tools, and Visio with the ability to quickly learn other tools as necessary.
• Business Tools - understanding and proficiency with business tools and technology, including Microsoft Office. The ideal candidate is advanced with Excel, Access, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word, and proficient with Adobe Acrobat, data analytic tools, and Visio with the ability to quickly learn other tools as necessary.
• Commitment - to work with smart, interesting people with diverse backgrounds to solve the biggest challenges across private, public and social sectors
• Curiosity - the ideal candidate exhibits an inquisitive nature and the ability to question the status quo among a community of people they enjoy and teams that work well together
• Humility - exhibits grace in success and failure while doing meaningful work where skills have impact and make a difference
• Willingness - to constantly learn, share, and grow and to view the world as their classroom

------------ ------------ ------------

BENEFITS AND HIGHLIGHTS

ProSidian Employee Benefits and Highlights: Your good health and well-being are important to ProSidian Consulting. At ProSidian, we invest in our employees to help them stay healthy and achieve work-life balance. That's why we are also pleased to offer the Employee Benefits Program, designed to promote your health and personal welfare. Our growing list of benefits currently include the following for Full Time Employees:

Competitive Compensation: Pay range begins in the competitive ranges with Group Health Benefits, Pre-tax Employee Benefits, and Performance Incentives. For medical and dental benefits, the Company contributes a fixed dollar amount each month towards the plan you elect. Contributions are deducted on a Pre-tax basis.

Group Medical Health Insurance Benefits: ProSidian partners with BC/BS, to offer a range of medical plans, including high-deductible health plans or PPOs. ||| Group Dental Health Insurance Benefits: ProSidian dental carriers - Delta, Aetna, Guardian, and MetLife.

Group Vision Health Insurance Benefits:ProSidian offers high/low vision plans through 2 carriers: Aetna and VSP.

401(k) Retirement Savings Plan: 401(k) Retirement Savings Plans help you save for your retirement for eligible employees. A range of investment options are available with a personal financial planner to assist you. The Plan is a pre-tax Safe Harbor 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan with a company match.

Vacation and Paid Time-Off (PTO) Benefits: Eligible employees use PTO for vacation, a doctor's appointment, or any number of events in your life. Currently these benefits include Vacation/Sick days - 2 weeks/3 days | Holidays - 10 ProSidian and Government Days are given.

Pre-Tax Payment Programs: Pre-Tax Payment Programs currently exist in the form of a Premium Only Plan (POP). These Plans offer a full Flexible Spending Account (FSA) Plan and a tax benefit for eligible employees.

Purchasing Discounts & Savings Plans: We want you to achieve financial success. We offer a Purchasing Discounts & Savings Plan through The Corporate Perks Benefit Program. This provides special discounts for eligible employees on products and services you buy on a daily basis.

Security Clearance: Due to the nature of our consulting engagements there are Security Clearance requirements for Engagement Teams handling sensitive Engagements in the Federal Marketplace. A Security Clearance is a valued asset in your professional portfolio and adds to your credentials.

ProSidian Employee & Contractor Referral Bonus Program: ProSidian Consulting will pay up to 5k for all referrals employed for 90 days for candidates submitted through our Referral Program.

Supplemental Life/Accidental Death and Dismemberment Insurance: If you want extra protection for yourself and your eligible dependents, you have the option to elect supplemental life insurance. D&D covers death or dismemberment from an accident only.

Short- and Long-Term Disability Insurance: Disability insurance plans are designed to provide income protection while you recover from a disability.

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

ProSidian Consulting has made a pledge to the Hiring Our Heroes Program of the U.S. Chamber of Commerce Foundation and the "I Hire Military" Initiative of The North Carolina Military Business Center (NCMBC) for the State of North Carolina. All applicants are encouraged to apply regardless of Veteran Status.

Furthermore, we believe in "HONOR ABOVE ALL" - be successful while doing things the right way. The pride comes out of the challenge; the reward is excellence in the work.

title: Executive Chef
description:
Company Overview

'Triple A Restaurant Group, is renowned for its commitment to culinary excellence and exceptional dining experiences. We pride ourselves on delivering high-quality food and outstanding service in a vibrant atmosphere.

Summary

We are seeking a talented and experienced Executive Chef to lead our culinary team at Triple A Group. This pivotal role involves overseeing all kitchen operations, ensuring the highest standards of food quality, safety, and presentation, and inspiring our culinary staff to excel. Join us to craft memorable dining experiences that reflect our passion for fine cuisine.

Responsibilities
• Lead and manage all kitchen operations, including food preparation, menu development, and staff supervision.
• Ensure compliance with health and safety regulations across all culinary activities.
• Oversee inventory control and food cost management to optimize profitability.
• Develop innovative menus that showcase our culinary excellence and cater to diverse customer preferences.
• Train, mentor, and motivate kitchen staff to foster a collaborative and high-performing team.
• Maintain the highest standards of food quality, presentation, and consistency.
• Coordinate with front-of-house staff to ensure seamless service delivery.
• Implement sustainable practices to reduce waste and improve efficiency.

Qualifications
• Proven 5 year experience as an Executive Chef or in a senior culinary leadership role within the restaurant industry.
• Extensive knowledge of fine dining cuisine and culinary techniques.
• Strong leadership skills with the ability to manage a diverse team.
• Excellent organizational skills with attention to detail.
• Ability to develop creative menus that appeal to a broad clientele.
• Knowledge of food safety regulations and best practices.
• Experience with inventory management and cost control.
• Exceptional communication skills.

Benefits apply after 90 days.

If you are passionate about leading culinary teams and creating exceptional dining experiences, we invite you to bring your expertise to Triple A. Embark on this flavorful journey with us—your next great adventure awaits!

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $60,000.00 - $70,000.00 per year

Ability to Commute:
• El Paso, TX 79901 (Required)

Ability to Relocate:
• El Paso, TX 79901: Relocate before starting work (Required)

Work Location: In person

title: Librarian
description:
Position Type:
Certified/Elementary School

Date Posted:
10/1/2025

Location:
FOR FUTURE VACANCIES

Closing Date:
03/31/2026
Manage the operation of the library and serve as a master teacher, materials expert, and technology/curriculum leader. Provide leadership in instructional strategies, library administration, material selection, and technology implementation and utilization. Adhere to federal and state laws, as well as, district policies, procedures and regulations.

Essential Functions
• Through the use of effective classroom management strategies, create a nurturing, safe library environment that is positive and conducive to learning and appropriate to the maturity level and interests of students.
• Participate in the curriculum development process to support academic achievement and utilize effective instructional strategies to teach information literacy, online safety, and the use of library materials to students.
• Support students in reading for different purposes and promote reading and inquiry learning as essential lifelong skills.
• Stay current on trends in librarianship in order to provide effective instruction to students on a variety of literacy skills.
• Lead and curate staff development for teachers on the availability and innovative use of digital/print resources and technology.
• Provide sources of information outside the physical library such as 24/7 online resources, digital content, and interlibrary loan.
• Collaborate with stakeholders when making decisions for classroom reading materials.
• Make recommendations for acquisitions and manage the processing, organizing, distribution, maintenance, and inventory of library media resources.
• Apply knowledge of collection development and utilize district policy in selecting materials and equipment for the school.
• Collaborate with the Innovative Technology Teacher (ITT) to deploy and maintain student devices and support the latest innovations in teaching and learning technology.
• Compile budget and estimate costs, maintain and file all reports, invoices, records, and other documents required based on program needs and state guidelines to evaluate program efficiency.
• Develop and coordinate an annual evaluation of the library media program, share with campus leadership, and make changes based on findings.
• Direct the work of library aides, student aides, and volunteers.
• Comply with campus, district, and state policies and procedures as appropriate.
• Attendance at the work site is required to meet essential functions of the job.
Other Responsibilities
• Participate in professional development activities as required by the district.
• Follow established safety procedures and techniques to perform job duties; support district/department goals.
• Maintain a professional code of ethics and perform other duties as assigned.
Virtual and Online Teaching Duties (additional as assigned)
• Plan and use appropriate instructional strategies, activities, and resources for distance or virtual learning that reflect an understanding of the learning styles and needs of assigned students according to guidelines established by Texas Education Agency, board policies, and administrative regulations.
• Be available by phone, email, or video conferencing during regular school business hours to confer with district personnel, students, and/or parents. Participate in meetings as required and/or coordinated by supervisors. Utilize the workday to plan, prepare, implement, and evaluate student work, lessons and activities.
• Communicate with students and parents for a minimum of one time per week via phone, email, website, educator communication app, or video/virtual conferencing.
• Respond to all correspondence from parents and students within the next business day of receipt.
• Create a virtual classroom environment conducive to learning and appropriate for the physical, social, and emotional development of students.
Mental Demands/Physical Demands/Environmental Factors:

Tools/Equipment Used: Personal or district issued computer, phone system, video/instructional equipment, and peripherals. Automated external defibrillator (AED) as assigned after receiving appropriate training.
Posture: Prolonged standing, sitting, frequent kneeling, squatting, bending, stooping, pushing, pulling and twisting
Motion: Repetitive computer work with frequent use of hands and wrists, frequent walking
Lifting: Regular lifting and carrying (less than 20 pounds); may lift and move books, stacks of books, text books, media equipment, desks and classroom equipment
Environment: Work inside, outside, from home or location other than school building, and exposure to noise
Mental Demands: Maintain emotional control under stress; work prolonged or irregular hours. Subject to the district's drug and alcohol screening policy and regulation.
Travel: Occasional district-wide travel and statewide travel. Texas or New Mexico driver's license; insurable by the district's auto liability insurance carrier.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree required and valid Texas school librarian certificate or endorsement, two years teaching experience, preferred. Must possess strong organizational, communications, computer, and public relations skills; possess excellent interpersonal & technological skills.

Teacher Salary Schedule
Click here to view Ysleta ISD's Compensation Plan

Required Documents:
The following are the required documents that must be submitted in order to be considered for an interview. You may attach the documents to your application or bring them into the YISD Central Office located at 9600 Sims Dr., El Paso, TX 79925. For more information, please contact the HR Department at 915-434-0428.

• Make recommendations for acquisitions and manage the processing, organizing, distribution, maintenance, and inventory of library media resources.
• Apply knowledge of collection development and utilize district policy in selecting materials and equipment for the school.
• Collaborate with the Innovative Technology Teacher (ITT) to deploy and maintain student devices and support the latest innovations in teaching and learning technology.
• Compile budget and estimate costs, maintain and file all reports, invoices, records, and other documents required based on program needs and state guidelines to evaluate program efficiency.
• Develop and coordinate an annual evaluation of the library media program, share with campus leadership, and make changes based on findings.
• Direct the work of library aides, student aides, and volunteers.
• Comply with campus, district, and state policies and procedures as appropriate.
• Attendance at the work site is required to meet essential functions of the job.
Other Responsibilities
• Participate in professional development activities as required by the district.
• Follow established safety procedures and techniques to perform job duties; support district/department goals.
• Maintain a professional code of ethics and perform other duties as assigned.
Virtual and Online Teaching Duties (additional as assigned)
• Plan and use appropriate instructional strategies, activities, and resources for distance or virtual learning that reflect an understanding of the learning styles and needs of assigned students according to guidelines established by Texas Education Agency, board policies, and administrative regulations.
• Be available by phone, email, or video conferencing during regular school business hours to confer with district personnel, students, and/or parents. Participate in meetings as required and/or coordinated by supervisors. Utilize the workday to plan, prepare, implement, and evaluate student work, lessons and activities.
• Communicate with students and parents for a minimum of one time per week via phone, email, website, educator communication app, or video/virtual conferencing.
• Respond to all correspondence from parents and students within the next business day of receipt.
• Create a virtual classroom environment conducive to learning and appropriate for the physical, social, and emotional development of students.
Mental Demands/Physical Demands/Environmental Factors:

Tools/Equipment Used: Personal or district issued computer, phone system, video/instructional equipment, and peripherals. Automated external defibrillator (AED) as assigned after receiving appropriate training.
Posture: Prolonged standing, sitting, frequent kneeling, squatting, bending, stooping, pushing, pulling and twisting
Motion: Repetitive computer work with frequent use of hands and wrists, frequent walking
Lifting: Regular lifting and carrying (less than 20 pounds); may lift and move books, stacks of books, text books, media equipment, desks and classroom equipment
Environment: Work inside, outside, from home or location other than school building, and exposure to noise
Mental Demands: Maintain emotional control under stress; work prolonged or irregular hours. Subject to the district's drug and alcohol screening policy and regulation.
Travel: Occasional district-wide travel and statewide travel. Texas or New Mexico driver's license; insurable by the district's auto liability insurance carrier.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree required and valid Texas school librarian certificate or endorsement, two years teaching experience, preferred. Must possess strong organizational, communications, computer, and public relations skills; possess excellent interpersonal & technological skills.

title: RN - Wound Care
description:
Bienvivir All-Inclusive Senior Health (“Bienvivir”) is a community-based, patient-centered, comprehensive health care delivery system that advocates and promotes quality of life, optimum independence, dignity, and choices in a nurturing environment for frail seniors. Since 1987, Bienvivir has served the frail seniors of El Paso, Texas through the provision of the Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (“PACE”).

PACE is a unique managed care benefit for frail seniors (referred to as participants) age 55 and older who are certified by the state as needing nursing home level care and who reside in a PACE service area. PACE programs coordinate and provide comprehensive medical and support services so that participants can remain independent and stay in their homes for as long as safely possible.

BENEFITS for Full and Part-time employees who work 30 or more hours per week:

We pay 100% of the MEDICAL monthly premiums for Employee Only coverage.

We pay 100% of the DENTAL monthly premiums for Employee Only coverage.

We provide an affordable VISION monthly premium for Employee + Family coverage.

We pay 100% of BASIC LIFE for a benefit amount of $10,000.

We offer safe harbor matching contributions for the 403(B) RETIREMENT SAVINGS account.

We offer up to fifteen (15) days of PAID TIME OFF based on paid hours per pay period.

We offer eleven (11) company-observed PAID HOLIDAYS.

We offer education and TUITION REIMBURSEMENT.

We offer MILEAGE REIMBURSEMENT.

Bienvivir is currently accepting applications for the following position:

WOUND CARE RN

The Wound Care Registered Nurse, under the supervision of the Director of Nursing, is responsible for overseeing Bienvivir’s wound care program. This role provides leadership and direction to LVNs in the treatment and management of wounds, ensuring evidence-based and participant-centered care. Key responsibilities include coordinating and tracking wound treatments, supervising data collection for quality improvement initiatives, and guiding staff in wound prevention and education. The Wound Care RN participates in care plan development, collaborates with the Primary Care Providers and the Interdisciplinary Team (IDT) in the care of participants with wounds, and supports data-driven decision-making to improve clinical outcomes.

RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Oversee the wound care program, including coordinating and monitoring wound care treatments provided by LVNs for all participants enrolled in Bienvivir.
• Supervise and support LVNs in the delivery of wound care, ensuring adherence to established protocols and standards of care.
• Provide direct wound care as needed, including making visits to participants’ places of residence.
• Ensure timely and effective wound care interventions through ongoing monitoring and coordination.
• Track wound progress and maintain accurate documentation in compliance with regulatory standards.
• Oversee data collection and reporting related to wound care for Quality Improvement initiatives.
• Lead the Wound Care Committee and actively participate in the Quality Improvement Committee and other committees as needed.
• Provide staff education on wound prevention, treatment protocols, and best practices.
• Support staff development by conducting general in-services, instruction, and orientation to the wound care program.
• Guide staff in obtaining, analyzing, and applying wound care data to support effective clinical decision making.
• Participate in individualized care plan development for participants with wounds.
• Collaborate with the Interdisciplinary Team (IDT) to ensure coordinated, participant-centered care.
• Collaborate and maintain ongoing communication with Primary Care Providers (PCPs) regarding assigned participants, ensuring they are kept informed of wound status, treatment progress, and any changes in condition. Verify that provider orders are obtained, documented, and carried out as directed.
• Assist in providing wound care.
• Evaluate the effectiveness of wound care strategies in relation to participant responses and expected outcomes.
• Other duties as assigned by the supervisor.

QUALIFICATIONS / REQUIREMENTS:
• Graduate of an accredited nursing program with a current license to practice as a Registered Nurse in the State of Texas.
• Valid wound care certification required.
• Current CPR certification is required.
• Minimum of two years of wound care experience required.
• Minimum of one year of experience with a frail or elderly population preferred.
• Valid Texas driver’s license and insurance.
• Bilingual (English/Spanish) preferred.

Company DescriptionOur mission is to provide the frail senior the Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE), a patient-centered, comprehensive healthcare delivery system in a community setting that advocates and promotes quality of life, optimum independence, dignity and choices in a nurturing environment.

Our staff are compassionate and observant professionals who strive to improve the quality of life for each of our participants. Apply online at www.bienvivir.org/employment

description:
Ready to take control of your schedule? Start your DirectCare Shifts application and you will be on your way to a better work- life balance in no time. Apply today, start tomorrow!

What you need:
LVN Licensed Vocational Nursing License to work in the State of Texas

Flexible Work Schedules:
• 8hr shift
• 12hr shift
• Day shift
• Night shift
• Overnight shift
• Weekends
• Monday to Friday
• Holidays
• Short-Term placement
• Long-Term placement

Why DirectCare Shifts?
At DirectCare Shifts we are empowering healthcare pro's to work on their terms. DirectCare Shiftsoffers a simplified approach to staffing. We match qualified healthcare pro's like you with shifts at facilities that we have partnered with. Do you want consistency? And guaranteed work? Consider our short-term or long-term placements. Do you prefer the flexibility and freedom of working with multiple facilities? We have per-diem shifts for you! No matter what your preferences are DirectCare Shifts has the platform to connect you with facilities in your area. With DirectCare Shifts, you'll get the pay, flexibility, and consistency that you deserve!

What you can expect from DirectCare Shifts:
• Next Day Pay options
• Weekly Direct Deposit
• Flexibility to pick your own schedule - No shift minimums
• PRN and Placement options available
• Referral Bonus Program
• Holiday and Hazard Pay
• Access to our Direct Care Pro Support Team who will be there to help you get through the hiring and credentialing process to your first shift and will stay with you during your time with us.

We look forward to working with you!

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

title: Dialysis Registered Nurse - El Paso, TX
description:
Company Description

ProSidian is a Management and Operations Consulting Services Firm focusing on providing value to clients through tailored solutions based on industry-leading practices. ProSidian services focus on the broad spectrum of Enterprise Solutions for Risk Management | Compliance | Business Process | IT Effectiveness | Energy & Sustainability | Human Capital. We help forward-thinking clients solve problems and improve operations.

Launched by former Big 4 Management Consultants; our multidisciplinary teams bring together the talents of nearly 190 professionals nationally to complete a wide variety of engagements for Private Companies, Fortune 1,000 Enterprises, and Government Agencies of all sizes. Our Services are deployed across the enterprise, target drivers of economic profit (growth, margin and efficiency), and are aligned at the intersections of assets, processes, policies and people delivering value.

ProSidian clients represent a broad spectrum of industries to include but are not limited to Energy, Manufacturing, Chemical, Retail, Healthcare, Telecommunications, Hospitality, Pharmaceuticals, Banking & Financial Services, Transportation, Federal and State Government Agencies. Learn More About ProSidian Consulting at www.ProSidian.com.

Job Description

Project Profile

The Veterans Integrated Services Network 17 (VISN 17) is a large network comprised of seven (7) healthcare systems geographically dispersed throughout the state of Texas. The VISN is supporting expanded bed capacity to meet the increased operational surge capacity throughout the state of Texas, and to support the VA's fourth mission by providing humanitarian assistance. Based on the current and projected future workload, additional staffing will be required over the next 24 months to support seven healthcare systems.

The Candidate

ProSidian seeks a Registered Nurse to support an engagement with The Veterans Integrated Services Network 17 (VISN 17). This position supports the current expanded operations and post-COVID operations to restore the healthcare systems to pre-Pandemic operations.

This is a Contract Contingent position and the Registered Nurse services shall be performed at the: El Paso VA Health Care System 5001 N Piedras St, El Paso, TX 79930.

Services Task and Job Requirements

In order to perform the duties set forth for this project, the Registered Nurse must meet both the minimum education and experience requirements set forth:

Qualifications

Education:

Graduated with a Bachelor of Science Nursing degree from a program that has been accredited by the Accreditation Commission for Education in Nursing (ACEN) or the Commission on Collegiate Nursing Education (CCNE).

Licensure:

Registered Nurse with a current Texas State license including BLS certification and ACLS certification.

Experience:
• Must demonstrate proficiency using the nursing process in for patients with complex nursing care needs and be able to guide and direct others who provide care.
• Must demonstrate leadership and be proficient at delivering hemodialysis effectively.
• Must be self-directed and comfortable working independently in the hemodialysis setting.
• Must have physical capabilities to initiate crisis intervention measures
• Must have the ability and knowledge to perform in nursing care emergencies.
• Must be able to serve as a charge nurse for hemodialysis unit as needed.
• Must be able to serve as float nurse in Hemodialysis Unit.
• Must be able to cover renal clinics as needed and provide patient education.
• Must have ability to serve as a liaison between Dialysis Unit (Chronic/Acute), including on-call schedule.
• Must be able to prepare and setup out-patients for TPA indwelling and monitoring.
• Must be able to initiate dialysis related IV infusions and monitor the patients.
• Must demonstrate effective oral and written communication skills and tact in dealing with patients, families and staff.
• Must demonstrate proficiency in keyboarding and basic computer operations.
• Additional duties may be assigned. Training will be provided if needed.

Additional Information

CORE COMPETENCIES
• Teamwork - ability to foster teamwork collaboratively as a participant, and effectively as a team leader
• Leadership - ability to guide and lead colleagues on projects and initiatives
• Business Acumen - understanding and insight into how organizations perform, including business processes, data, systems, and people
• Communication - ability to effectively communicate to stakeholders of all levels orally and in writing
• Motivation - persistent in pursuit of quality and optimal client and company solutions
• Agility - ability to quickly understand and transition between different projects, concepts, initiatives, or work streams
• Judgment - exercises prudence and insight in decision-making process while mindful of other stakeholders and long-term ramifications
• Organization - ability to manage projects and activity, and prioritize tasks

------------ ------------ ------------

OTHER REQUIREMENTS
• Business Tools - understanding and proficiency with business tools and technology, including Microsoft Office. The ideal candidate is advanced with Excel, Access, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word, and proficient with Adobe Acrobat, data analytic tools, and Visio with the ability to quickly learn other tools as necessary.
• Business Tools - understanding and proficiency with business tools and technology, including Microsoft Office. The ideal candidate is advanced with Excel, Access, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word, and proficient with Adobe Acrobat, data analytic tools, and Visio with the ability to quickly learn other tools as necessary.
• Commitment - to work with smart, interesting people with diverse backgrounds to solve the biggest challenges across private, public and social sectors
• Curiosity - the ideal candidate exhibits an inquisitive nature and the ability to question the status quo among a community of people they enjoy and teams that work well together
• Humility - exhibits grace in success and failure while doing meaningful work where skills have impact and make a difference
• Willingness - to constantly learn, share, and grow and to view the world as their classroom

------------ ------------ ------------

BENEFITS AND HIGHLIGHTS

ProSidian Employee Benefits and Highlights: Your good health and well-being are important to ProSidian Consulting. At ProSidian, we invest in our employees to help them stay healthy and achieve work-life balance. That's why we are also pleased to offer the Employee Benefits Program, designed to promote your health and personal welfare. Our growing list of benefits currently include the following for Full Time Employees:

Competitive Compensation: Pay range begins in the competitive ranges with Group Health Benefits, Pre-tax Employee Benefits, and Performance Incentives. For medical and dental benefits, the Company contributes a fixed dollar amount each month towards the plan you elect. Contributions are deducted on a Pre-tax basis.

Group Medical Health Insurance Benefits: ProSidian partners with BC/BS, to offer a range of medical plans, including high-deductible health plans or PPOs. ||| Group Dental Health Insurance Benefits: ProSidian dental carriers - Delta, Aetna, Guardian, and MetLife.

Group Vision Health Insurance Benefits:ProSidian offers high/low vision plans through 2 carriers: Aetna and VSP.

401(k) Retirement Savings Plan: 401(k) Retirement Savings Plans help you save for your retirement for eligible employees. A range of investment options are available with a personal financial planner to assist you. The Plan is a pre-tax Safe Harbor 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan with a company match.

Vacation and Paid Time-Off (PTO) Benefits: Eligible employees use PTO for vacation, a doctor's appointment, or any number of events in your life. Currently these benefits include Vacation/Sick days - 2 weeks/3 days | Holidays - 10 ProSidian and Government Days are given.

Pre-Tax Payment Programs: Pre-Tax Payment Programs currently exist in the form of a Premium Only Plan (POP). These Plans offer a full Flexible Spending Account (FSA) Plan and a tax benefit for eligible employees.

Purchasing Discounts & Savings Plans: We want you to achieve financial success. We offer a Purchasing Discounts & Savings Plan through The Corporate Perks Benefit Program. This provides special discounts for eligible employees on products and services you buy on a daily basis.

Security Clearance: Due to the nature of our consulting engagements there are Security Clearance requirements for Engagement Teams handling sensitive Engagements in the Federal Marketplace. A Security Clearance is a valued asset in your professional portfolio and adds to your credentials.

ProSidian Employee & Contractor Referral Bonus Program: ProSidian Consulting will pay up to 5k for all referrals employed for 90 days for candidates submitted through our Referral Program.

Performance Incentives: Due to the nature of our consulting engagements there are performance incentives associated with each new client that each employee works to pursue and support.

Flexible Spending Account: FSAs help you pay for eligible out-of-pocket health care and dependent daycare expenses on a pre-tax basis. You determine your projected expenses for the Plan Year and then elect to set aside a portion of each paycheck into your FSA.

Supplemental Life/Accidental Death and Dismemberment Insurance: If you want extra protection for yourself and your eligible dependents, you have the option to elect supplemental life insurance. D&D covers death or dismemberment from an accident only.

Short- and Long-Term Disability Insurance: Disability insurance plans are designed to provide income protection while you recover from a disability.

raw_payload.job_description:
Company Description

ProSidian is a Management and Operations Consulting Services Firm focusing on providing value to clients through tailored solutions based on industry-leading practices. ProSidian services focus on the broad spectrum of Enterprise Solutions for Risk Management | Compliance | Business Process | IT Effectiveness | Energy & Sustainability | Human Capital. We help forward-thinking clients solve problems and improve operations.

Launched by former Big 4 Management Consultants; our multidisciplinary teams bring together the talents of nearly 190 professionals nationally to complete a wide variety of engagements for Private Companies, Fortune 1,000 Enterprises, and Government Agencies of all sizes. Our Services are deployed across the enterprise, target drivers of economic profit (growth, margin and efficiency), and are aligned at the intersections of assets, processes, policies and people delivering value.

ProSidian clients represent a broad spectrum of industries to include but are not limited to Energy, Manufacturing, Chemical, Retail, Healthcare, Telecommunications, Hospitality, Pharmaceuticals, Banking & Financial Services, Transportation, Federal and State Government Agencies. Learn More About ProSidian Consulting at www.ProSidian.com.

Job Description

Project Profile

The Veterans Integrated Services Network 17 (VISN 17) is a large network comprised of seven (7) healthcare systems geographically dispersed throughout the state of Texas. The VISN is supporting expanded bed capacity to meet the increased operational surge capacity throughout the state of Texas, and to support the VA's fourth mission by providing humanitarian assistance. Based on the current and projected future workload, additional staffing will be required over the next 24 months to support seven healthcare systems.

The Candidate

ProSidian seeks a Registered Nurse to support an engagement with The Veterans Integrated Services Network 17 (VISN 17). This position supports the current expanded operations and post-COVID operations to restore the healthcare systems to pre-Pandemic operations.

This is a Contract Contingent position and the Registered Nurse services shall be performed at the: El Paso VA Health Care System 5001 N Piedras St, El Paso, TX 79930.

Services Task and Job Requirements

In order to perform the duties set forth for this project, the Registered Nurse must meet both the minimum education and experience requirements set forth:

Qualifications

Education:

Graduated with a Bachelor of Science Nursing degree from a program that has been accredited by the Accreditation Commission for Education in Nursing (ACEN) or the Commission on Collegiate Nursing Education (CCNE).

Licensure:

Registered Nurse with a current Texas State license including BLS certification and ACLS certification.

Experience:
• Must demonstrate proficiency using the nursing process in for patients with complex nursing care needs and be able to guide and direct others who provide care.
• Must demonstrate leadership and be proficient at delivering hemodialysis effectively.
• Must be self-directed and comfortable working independently in the hemodialysis setting.
• Must have physical capabilities to initiate crisis intervention measures
• Must have the ability and knowledge to perform in nursing care emergencies.
• Must be able to serve as a charge nurse for hemodialysis unit as needed.
• Must be able to serve as float nurse in Hemodialysis Unit.
• Must be able to cover renal clinics as needed and provide patient education.
• Must have ability to serve as a liaison between Dialysis Unit (Chronic/Acute), including on-call schedule.
• Must be able to prepare and setup out-patients for TPA indwelling and monitoring.
• Must be able to initiate dialysis related IV infusions and monitor the patients.
• Must demonstrate effective oral and written communication skills and tact in dealing with patients, families and staff.
• Must demonstrate proficiency in keyboarding and basic computer operations.
• Additional duties may be assigned. Training will be provided if needed.

Additional Information

CORE COMPETENCIES
• Teamwork - ability to foster teamwork collaboratively as a participant, and effectively as a team leader
• Leadership - ability to guide and lead colleagues on projects and initiatives
• Business Acumen - understanding and insight into how organizations perform, including business processes, data, systems, and people
• Communication - ability to effectively communicate to stakeholders of all levels orally and in writing
• Motivation - persistent in pursuit of quality and optimal client and company solutions
• Agility - ability to quickly understand and transition between different projects, concepts, initiatives, or work streams
• Judgment - exercises prudence and insight in decision-making process while mindful of other stakeholders and long-term ramifications
• Organization - ability to manage projects and activity, and prioritize tasks

------------ ------------ ------------

OTHER REQUIREMENTS
• Business Tools - understanding and proficiency with business tools and technology, including Microsoft Office. The ideal candidate is advanced with Excel, Access, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word, and proficient with Adobe Acrobat, data analytic tools, and Visio with the ability to quickly learn other tools as necessary.
• Business Tools - understanding and proficiency with business tools and technology, including Microsoft Office. The ideal candidate is advanced with Excel, Access, Outlook, PowerPoint and Word, and proficient with Adobe Acrobat, data analytic tools, and Visio with the ability to quickly learn other tools as necessary.
• Commitment - to work with smart, interesting people with diverse backgrounds to solve the biggest challenges across private, public and social sectors
• Curiosity - the ideal candidate exhibits an inquisitive nature and the ability to question the status quo among a community of people they enjoy and teams that work well together
• Humility - exhibits grace in success and failure while doing meaningful work where skills have impact and make a difference
• Willingness - to constantly learn, share, and grow and to view the world as their classroom

------------ ------------ ------------

BENEFITS AND HIGHLIGHTS

ProSidian Employee Benefits and Highlights: Your good health and well-being are important to ProSidian Consulting. At ProSidian, we invest in our employees to help them stay healthy and achieve work-life balance. That's why we are also pleased to offer the Employee Benefits Program, designed to promote your health and personal welfare. Our growing list of benefits currently include the following for Full Time Employees:

Competitive Compensation: Pay range begins in the competitive ranges with Group Health Benefits, Pre-tax Employee Benefits, and Performance Incentives. For medical and dental benefits, the Company contributes a fixed dollar amount each month towards the plan you elect. Contributions are deducted on a Pre-tax basis.

Group Medical Health Insurance Benefits: ProSidian partners with BC/BS, to offer a range of medical plans, including high-deductible health plans or PPOs. ||| Group Dental Health Insurance Benefits: ProSidian dental carriers - Delta, Aetna, Guardian, and MetLife.

Group Vision Health Insurance Benefits:ProSidian offers high/low vision plans through 2 carriers: Aetna and VSP.

401(k) Retirement Savings Plan: 401(k) Retirement Savings Plans help you save for your retirement for eligible employees. A range of investment options are available with a personal financial planner to assist you. The Plan is a pre-tax Safe Harbor 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan with a company match.

Vacation and Paid Time-Off (PTO) Benefits: Eligible employees use PTO for vacation, a doctor's appointment, or any number of events in your life. Currently these benefits include Vacation/Sick days - 2 weeks/3 days | Holidays - 10 ProSidian and Government Days are given.

Pre-Tax Payment Programs: Pre-Tax Payment Programs currently exist in the form of a Premium Only Plan (POP). These Plans offer a full Flexible Spending Account (FSA) Plan and a tax benefit for eligible employees.

Purchasing Discounts & Savings Plans: We want you to achieve financial success. We offer a Purchasing Discounts & Savings Plan through The Corporate Perks Benefit Program. This provides special discounts for eligible employees on products and services you buy on a daily basis.

Security Clearance: Due to the nature of our consulting engagements there are Security Clearance requirements for Engagement Teams handling sensitive Engagements in the Federal Marketplace. A Security Clearance is a valued asset in your professional portfolio and adds to your credentials.

ProSidian Employee & Contractor Referral Bonus Program: ProSidian Consulting will pay up to 5k for all referrals employed for 90 days for candidates submitted through our Referral Program.

Performance Incentives: Due to the nature of our consulting engagements there are performance incentives associated with each new client that each employee works to pursue and support.

Flexible Spending Account: FSAs help you pay for eligible out-of-pocket health care and dependent daycare expenses on a pre-tax basis. You determine your projected expenses for the Plan Year and then elect to set aside a portion of each paycheck into your FSA.

Supplemental Life/Accidental Death and Dismemberment Insurance: If you want extra protection for yourself and your eligible dependents, you have the option to elect supplemental life insurance. D&D covers death or dismemberment from an accident only.

Short- and Long-Term Disability Insurance: Disability insurance plans are designed to provide income protection while you recover from a disability.

------------ ------------ ------------

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION - See Below Instructions On The Best Way To Apply

ProSidian Consulting is an equal opportunity employer and considers qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, age, disability, or Vietnam era, or other eligible veteran status, or any other protected factor. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

ProSidian Consulting has made a pledge to the Hiring Our Heroes Program of the U.S. Chamber of Commerce Foundation and the "I Hire Military" Initiative of The North Carolina Military Business Center (NCMBC) for the State of North Carolina. All applicants are encouraged to apply regardless of Veteran Status.

Furthermore, we believe in "HONOR ABOVE ALL" - be successful while doing things the right way. The pride comes out of the challenge; the reward is excellence in the work.

Be sure to place the job reference code in the subject line of your email. Be sure to include your name, address, telephone number, total compensation package, employment history, and educational credentials.

title: Other Positions - El Paso
description:
Hello,

If you are an experienced AEC industry professional and didn't see a position that fits but you'd still like to send us your resume, cover letter and portfolio, please do that here. Students looking for internships should not apply here: Students should only apply when internship/co-op positions are posted.

NOTE: This process will involve you uploading a cover letter. Please do not upload your resume twice or some other document. We need to know why you're sending us your application, what type of position you're interested in and why we should consider creating an opening for you. The cover letter is the perfect place to help us hire you.

As a government subcontractor we're required by the OFCCP to provide applicants with the ability to voluntarily Self Identify as an individual with a disability. If you'd like to do this you can find the form here: https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/OFCCP/regs/compliance/sec503/Self_ID_Forms/503Self-IDForm.pdf Simply include this form with your application.

Thanks

title: Drums instructor needed in El Paso, TX
description:
Job Description:
Do you bring out the star in your students?

Our motto here at Taylor Robinson Music is "Learn to Play From the Heart", and we are looking for private music instructors who share the same philosophy! We strive to make music lessons as accessible and affordable to as many people as possible because we believe the importance of music education is essential to making the world a better, happier place!

Taylor Robinson Music is hiring private music instructors, especially for Drums. We do not have a brick-and-mortar studio location in the El Paso, TX area, but we have students needing teachers ASAP!

Lessons can occur in the students' homes; in your own home or your own studio/rehearsal space; or via webcam. Please note that while we do get requests for webcam, they make up only a small percentage of our student base. Most of our students still prefer in-person lessons, so we need local instructors who can host students and/or are willing to travel to students in the El Paso, TX area.

You would be paid on an independent contractor basis between $20-$30 per hour dependent on travel time, your experience/education, and your student retention rate. You have the freedom to set your own schedule, as well as your own curriculum/lesson plans! Also, we take care of all the advertising, marketing, and SEO work for you to bring the students in and handle all the billing to help lighten your load so you can focus on what's most important - the music!

If you are great with kids, love teaching, keep in good communication with our office, and are organized and on time for all lessons, Taylor Robinson Music might be the place. Formal music training is not mandatory but EITHER a degree in music, enrollment in a music program, professional performing experience, or previous teaching experience is a must.

If you have one of the above and are interested in learning more, please apply using the link below:

www.taylorrobinsonmusic.com/teach-music

Are you taking any additional COVID-19 precautions?

Though some students are okay to take lessons via webcam, most students are hoping to go to their instructor's studio or have their instructor come to their home. You must wear a mask, sanitize your hands, & practice social distancing when applicable.

1 year (Required)

title: HOME BASED INSURANCE SALES/WORK FROM HOME
description:
We are looking for individuals interested in working from home, remotely, as life insurance sales representatives. We are hiring coachable individuals comfortable with a 100% commission based income helping our clients who have asked for our help with life insurance backed products. Agents usually help 3 - 5 families each week, and average $300 - $500 for each family they help. You must be a US citizen to qualify for this position. SCHEDULE AN INTERVIEW TODAY!
As this is a commission based income, there is no cap on your earnings. We use data driven systems and cutting edge lead generation that gets you connected with interested clients quickly.
The candidate we are looking for is disciplined, honest, confident, and passionate about helping people achieve their financial goals. Occasional travel for work for in-person conferences. If you are not currently licensed but have a desire to learn this business, we will help guide you in that process.

Sales Job Description:
• Call on our lead prospects to set up appointments.
• Help each client to review their options and apply for that coverage.
• See the application through the underwriting process and get our clients covered.

Requirements for Sales Position:
• Must be licensed in life products or willing to get licensed.
• Must have a computer and phone to service the clients.
• This is all online so internet connection is a must.
• We provide all of the training.
• We have warm leads available who have contacted us first. No COLD calling.
• Must be a US citizen.

We provide:
• Training
• Mentorship
• Lead system for getting in front of clients

If you are interested in learning more about working with us, please SCHEDULE AN INTERVIEW TODAY!

title: Nurse Practitioner - Internal Medicine / Cardiology - El Paso, TX
description:
Job Summary

Tenet Healthcare is seeking an experienced Nurse Practitioner to join an Internal Medicine practice in El Paso, Texas. This full-time outpatient position offers an excellent opportunity to provide comprehensive adult primary care and provide additional coverage for Cardiology patients as well.

Position Highlights:
• Full-time schedule: Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM
• 100% outpatient-based
• No nights, weekends, or call responsibilities
• Join a supportive, independent practice setting with an experienced supervising physician
• Responsibilities include routine follow-ups, chronic disease management, wellness visits, and acute care

Compensation and Benefits:
• Competitive compensation package with productivity bonus
• Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits
• Paid time off (PTO) and CME allowance
• 401(k) retirement plan
• Malpractice insurance coverage

El Paso offers a vibrant blend of culture, natural beauty, and affordability. Nestled along the Rio Grande and surrounded by mountain views, the city provides a warm climate and an active lifestyle year-round. With excellent schools, low cost of living, and a strong sense of community, El Paso is an exceptional place to live and work.

title: Outpatient No Call No Weekends Geriatric Physician Opportunity in El Paso Texas
description:
Salary: $265k - 330k per year
Reference: JN -102025-34449

Geriatrics in El Paso, Texas

Join an outpatient geriatrics practice in El Paso where you can focus on caring for older adults in a collaborative, well-supported environment. This role offers schedule flexibility, strong team resources, and the opportunity to help shape a dedicated geriatrics clinic as the program continues to grow.

Practice Details
• Outpatient-only geriatrics care with no inpatient responsibilities
• High-volume clinic seeing approximately 14 to 18 patients per day
• Multidisciplinary support, including nurses, medical assistants, social workers, and a clinical pharmacist
• Modern outpatient setting with exam rooms, on-site pharmacy, imaging, lab, and attached emergency department
• Opportunity to contribute to the development of a new geriatrics clinic and future leadership roles

Schedule
• Monday through Friday outpatient schedule
• Option for a four-day workweek
• No weekends

Compensation & Benefits
• Guaranteed base salary with additional incentives
• Sign-on bonus and relocation assistance available
• Full benefits package including malpractice coverage
• CME allowance with paid CME time

Candidate Requirements
• Fellowship-trained in Geriatrics or significant geriatrics experience in Family Medicine or Internal Medicine
• Spanish-speaking preferred but not required; interpreter services and AI-supported tools available
• Values independence while working within a team-based care model

About El Paso, Texas

El Paso offers a welcoming community with a low cost of living, easy commutes, and a warm climate year-round. The city features a rich cultural scene, strong ties to the outdoors with nearby mountains and desert trails, and convenient access to a regional airport. It is an appealing place for physicians seeking meaningful work and a balanced lifestyle.

Apply today to be part of a growing geriatrics program and help shape care for older adults in the El Paso community.
